entry.title: Remove CDP Class from Product
entry.content:
How can i remove CDP class from Material in PPDS/APO?  I want to change CDP class.

So far I have tried deactivating Advance Planning from material, removing old class then assign new class and reactive material for Advance Planning but still old class is showing in MAT1 classification tab.

BR

entry.title: test 2
Onboarding/Crossboarding/Offboarding - Save Draft Option?
entry.content:
During the onboarding, crossboarding, and offboarding processes, HR Operations must complete numerous tasks related to documentation, updates, and other administrative activities. These tasks vary depending on the specific reason for the process.

Since we want to avoid creating separate custom tasks for each individual activity, we considered using a single custom task that would include a checklist of all required actions. However, the problem is that the task cannot be temporarily saved while being updated — the only available option is “Send”.

Has anyone encountered a similar situation, and how did you resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Exposing application service running in Kyma cluster using APIRule
entry.content:
Thanks @gabbi !
Well it is exposed but it is not working. 
When we deploy the service it looks ready from Kyma dashboard but the Status regarding external IP and URL is missing.

entry.title: Re: How can I allow a communication user to schedule a ba...
entry.content:
This behavior is by design. The Application Job Framework (in SAP BTP ABAP Environment) is intended only for business users, not for technical users like communication users.

Authorization objects such as S_START and S_APJ* authorizations protect only the technical channel (the ability to use application jobs at all). They do not cover the business authorizations required by the logic executed inside an application job. Since application jobs can run arbitrary business code (LoB, partner, or custom logic), they must run in the context of a business user who holds the necessary business roles and authorizations. A communication user cannot hold these.

Allowing a technical user to schedule application jobs would enable business data changes without proper business authorization checks or auditability, which is why this is intentionally restricted.

The supported pattern is to use the standard External Scheduler Integration (SAP_COM_0326). An external system calls an inbound OData API and provides a business user as input. The framework then schedules the application job on behalf of that business user, via the use of an authorized Business User it is than ensured that correct authorizations are present to perform all relevant business processess and the process can be traced and audited via this business user.

As a final remark, it may be worth asking whether an application job is really what is needed in the first place.

An application job implies repetitive execution of code along a time-based pattern, but many scenarios are only interested in asynchronous or background processing instead of dialog processing. In such cases, other frameworks might be more appropriate.

For example, the Background Processing Framework (bgPF) focuses on background execution without the concept of repetitive scheduling and may already be sufficient for these use cases. It is seen as an anti-pattern to use one-time / immediate application jobs in architectures where a simple background processing or asynchronous call is sufficient to achieve the same result - as it uncessessarily decreases overall availability of batch resources.

I have not compared this in detail, but if the requirement is “background over dialog” rather than “recurring time-based execution,” this could be an alternative worth exploring — potentially also with communication users.

Documentation:
SAP_COM_0326 - External Scheduler Integration
Inbound OData service to enable external systems to schedule an application job based on an application job template.
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/integrating-external-scheduler?locale=en-US 

Background Processing Framework
The background processing framework (bgPF) is a framework that asynchronously and reliably executes methods of applications that develop background processes.
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/background-processing-framework?locale=en-US

entry.title: How can we replicate wbs elements from S4 to Fieldglass
entry.content:
We are using SAP Integration Suite to integrate SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fieldglass.

How can we replicate WBS elements from S/4HANA to Fieldglass?
Is there any communication scenario available for this purpose other than SAP_COM_0111?

Additionally, we would like to replicate the Planned Start Date and Planned Finish Date.

However, SAP_COM_0111 does not support these fields.

entry.title: Re: Peso liquido e bruto na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica - Brazil
entry.content:
Hi,

We face to the same problem.

Please anyone has a suggestion ?

entry.title: Re: Transport request/save locally popup not shown anymore in F2814 Manage KPI
entry.content:
Don't click Edit.

Instead, click the button Undo:

As you can see, the changes are gone (no pen in front of currency, value reverted back to original USD currency, no warning anymore about "Input Parameters changed"):

Now, you may click Edit, do your change and click Save and Activate, the popup is shown again:

That's it.

For information, if you see the popup without "Save as a Local Object" and a given transport request selected (as below), it's because there is an open transport request containing the KPI:

(/SSB/EVALS, table of extensions; ID: extension ID; REF_ID: ID of the standard KPI modified whose definition can be found in the table /SSB/EVAL (without trailing "S")).

entry.title: Whether SAP standard milestone billing supports partial billing against a single financial milestone
entry.content:
Please advise on:

1.Whether SAP standard milestone billing supports partial billing against a single financial milestone with clear visibility of:

  • Billed vs. pending milestone value

2.If configuration or enhancement is required to:

  • Track milestone-wise billing status
  • Report remaining unbilled value against the same milestone

3.Recommended best practice in SAP PS–SD integration for this scenario.

4.Is material delivery possible in a milestone-based billing scenario?

5.Is partial delivery of BOM components possible? For example, if a BOM contains 10 components, can 5 components be delivered initially and the remaining 5 delivered later?

entry.title: Is ADS stateless?
entry.content:
Hello, we have question about ADS usage:

Exists 2 independent ADS servers SID1 and SID2 (not one SID with CI+AS). We want to balance incomming load (and have some server redundancy) via webdispatcher (kernel 7.54) and parameter "wdisp/system_conflict_resolution=ROUND_ROBIN". This parameter exists as valid in documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_AS_ABAP_752/683d6a1797a34730a6e005d1e8de6f22/b0ebfa88e9164d26bdf1d21a7ef6fc25.html?locale=en-US with remark "Keep in mind that then all requests must be stateless.". Is not clear if ADS requests are stateless or not (My feeling is - should be). Can you answer this question?

Thank you, Martin

entry.title: Re: Missing Field - Manage Supplier Line Items App
entry.content:
Good day @Mohit2212 

Thank you for your question.

I think it is important to understand what the public cloud offers you in your scenario. 

  • You can only show fields that the app already exposes through view setting and then you have the option to save your changes as a variant. This is standard.
  • However, it is not possible to carry out “Check Number” if it is not in the Columns list. You cannot add it to Manage Supplier Line Items with Adapt UI or by configuring. Public cloud simply does not provide that.

The standard ways I suggest is, in public cloud, you should display it through “Manage Outgoing Checks” or through navigation from your payment document and not inside the supplier line item list. See Outgoing Checks,  Manage Outgoing Checks and Manage Supplier Line Items for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 3: Rapid Conversion Strategies
entry.content:
Co-Author: Dennis Leslie

Wondering how challenging it really is to convert your existing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape to SAP Central Business Configuration and the 3-system landscape? Check out this impressive success story:

dsm-firmenich's i-Health division, in collaboration with SAP partner HCLTech and SAP, completed their conversion to the 3-system landscape in a remarkable 23 days. Samantha Bates, project manager at HCLTech, shares their secrets to success:
- Following the SAP Activate roadmap for conversion
- Leveraging tools like the Cloud Integration Automation service and test data refresh

Also in this video: Ancuta Mezinca from SAP’s conversion customer office explains the key differences between the 2-system and 3-system landscape.

Expert talk with HCLTech (Part 1)

Stay tuned for our next blog posts in this blog series and explore the power of the 3-system landscape today! To learn more, check out the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape page on SAP Community.

If you liked this blog, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: Re: SAP Central Business Configuration – Blog Collection
entry.content: New blog alert − Check out our latest blog post: "Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 3: Rapid Conversion Strategies"

entry.title: Re: Workforce replication from SuccessFactors to S4/Hana Public Cloud using Master Data Integration
entry.content:
Hey @Kirthan_Kumar, 

this is Carlo from the SAP Master Data Integration Team. It is great to see content around our product - thanks for putting the effort!

We found this article when working on a customer ticket. One of our customers was confused because of the phrasing around service instances and service keys. The concrete issue they ran into, was that they only created one service instance and used it for connecting SuccessFactors and S/4. Could you adjust the phrasing, that that it becomes clearer, that it is required to setup one service instance per connected application?

Concrete proposals: Section1:

Create the instance and service key for Master Data Integration. -> Create the instances and service keys for Master Data Integration.

Create another key for S4C application with application "s4" -> Create another service instance for S4C application with application "s4"

 

Section2:

Add the the uri you received from the 1st step of the BTP after creating the service key for sfsf business system. -> Add the the uri you received from the service key of the service instance which you created for sfsf in the 1st step.

 

Section 3:

host - uri from s4 service key of BTP. -> host - uri from the service key of the service instance which was created for S4 in BTP.

OAuth 2.0 Client ID - Value from clientid in the s4 service key -> Value from clientid in the service key of the service instance which was created for S4 in BTP

Thanks!

   Carlo

entry.title: Why Source‑to‑Pay Excellence Defines Modern Procurement Leadership
entry.content:
Introduction: Setting the Stage

Procurement leaders today operate in a landscape defined by global volatility, sustainability expectations, and the need for continuous operational efficiency. Against this backdrop, Source‑to‑Pay (S2P) stands out as a holistic and strategic framework that brings structure, visibility, and integration to every stage of buying goods and services.

This article examines the full S2P lifecycle—from identifying needs through paying suppliers—highlighting why end‑to‑end visibility matters, what challenges organisations commonly encounter, and how digital tools such as SAP ERP and SAP Ariba strengthen procurement performance. Grounded in real‑world examples, this blog illustrates how a complete S2P model drives measurable business outcomes while creating the foundation for long‑term value.

The Source‑to‑Pay (S2P) Lifecycle: A Truly End‑to‑End View

The Source‑to‑Pay process encompasses every activity required to purchase and pay for goods or services. Unlike Procure‑to‑Pay (P2P), which begins after a supplier is already selected, or Source‑to‑Contract (S2C), which ends once the contract is awarded, S2P integrates sourcing, contracting, ordering, supplier performance, and payment into one coherent flow.

Key Steps in the S2P Process

  • Identifying Needs: Departments articulate what they require and initiate the sourcing conversation.
  • Supplier Identification & Selection: Procurement evaluates options, negotiates terms, and ensures compliance—often supported by e‑sourcing tools.
  • Contract Management: Organisations formalise terms, mitigate risks, and establish performance expectations before any ordering begins.
  • Ordering: Purchase orders are created and shared digitally to ensure accuracy and traceability.
  • Receiving Goods or Services: Deliveries are validated for quality and alignment to the PO.
  • Supplier Performance Management: Teams monitor delivery reliability, quality, and compliance using dashboards and analytics.
  • Payment: Invoices are matched and processed, increasingly through automated or e‑invoicing solutions.

Many basic S2P outlines omit it or treat it as an ongoing activity rather than a step. Monitoring supplier delivery reliability, quality, and compliance (often via dashboards and KPIs) is very much aligned with current best practices in Supplier Relationship Management (SRM). One comment: this “step” usually occurs continuously or periodically (e.g. quarterly performance reviews) rather than a one-time event after each delivery. So, it might be seen as an overarching practice throughout the supplier lifecycle.

The Holistic Nature of S2P

S2P bridges the gap between strategic sourcing and transactional procurement, providing visibility, consistency, and control across the entire lifecycle. This end‑to‑end structure is what allows organisations to scale procurement excellence and build reliable, data‑driven processes.

Why S2P Matters

S2P is not merely an operational workflow—it is a strategic enabler. By digitising and connecting all procurement stages, organisations can:

  • increase transparency
  • strengthen risk management
  • improve supplier relationships
  • accelerate cycle times
  • ensure compliance

Modern S2P environments use technologies such as e‑sourcing, supplier portals, e‑invoicing, and real‑time analytics to deliver accuracy and efficiency across the value chain. When these tools work as one, procurement becomes a strategic powerhouse rather than a transactional function.

Common Procurement Challenges Across the S2P Journey

1. Lack of Spend Visibility

Fragmented systems or manual spreadsheets often lead to incomplete or inaccurate spend insights. Without full visibility, organisations cannot leverage scale, consolidate suppliers, or negotiate effectively.

2. Manual, Inefficient Processes

Paper‑based workflows delay approvals, increase errors, and disrupt the procurement rhythm—from sourcing all the way to invoicing.

3. Weak Supplier Management

Without consistent monitoring across selection, contracting, delivery, and performance, organisations face reliability issues, quality shortfalls, and contractual risk.

4. Compliance & Risk Gaps

Global procurement environments heighten exposure to non‑compliance, fraud, and supply disruptions when controls are not embedded end‑to‑end.

5. Data Quality & Integration Issues

Siloed or inaccurate data prevents robust analytics, demand forecasting, and the effective deployment of digital procurement tools.

Common Procurement Outcomes: What Strong S2P Execution Delivers

Cost Savings

Consolidated spend and improved supplier management help organisations negotiate better deals and eliminate waste.

Efficiency Gains

Automating procurement steps—from sourcing to invoice matching—reduces errors and accelerates cycle times.

Improved Supplier Relationships

Transparent, data‑driven performance monitoring supports stronger, more collaborative supplier partnerships.

Compliance and Risk Control

Integrated policies and controls reduce exposure to fraud, contractual deviations, and supply chain disruptions.

Data‑Driven Decision‑Making

Unified procurement data powers analytics, reporting, and continuous improvement across the entire lifecycle.

Real‑World Example: European Manufacturer Achieves 95% Spend Visibility

A large European manufacturing company discovered that only 65% of its €900 million annual procurement spend was visible across the S2P cycle. Data was fragmented between sourcing, ordering, and payment systems, limiting leverage for supplier consolidation and cost optimisation.

By implementing an integrated digital spend analytics layer across all stages—from supplier selection to invoicing—the company increased spend visibility to 95%. With a unified view, the procurement team standardised processes and identified consolidation opportunities, ultimately delivering €12 million in annual cost savings and strengthening supplier relationships.

 

How SAP ERP and SAP Ariba Enable Source‑to‑Pay Excellence

Cost Reduction & Spend Visibility

SAP Ariba’s spend analytics software unify sourcing, contracting, and purchasing data, enabling organisations to detect duplicates, consolidate suppliers, and negotiate more effectively.

Process Efficiency

Automated workflows in SAP ERP and SAP Ariba accelerate requisitioning, approvals, ordering, and invoice matching, reducing manual workload and errors.

Supplier Management & Collaboration

Centralised supplier data and collaboration portals support end‑to‑end performance management and risk monitoring.

Compliance & Risk Management

Integrated risk assessment tools enforce policy adherence and help organisations address disruptions proactively.

Data Integration & Strategic Insight

SAP ERP and SAP Ariba connect procurement with finance, inventory, and analytics, ensuring accurate, end‑to‑end data for reporting and strategic planning.

Together, these solutions transform procurement from fragmented tasks into a transparent, agile, and value‑driven enterprise discipline.

 

Conclusion: Closing the Loop

As highlighted in the introduction, procurement must operate with clarity, integration, and strategic intent to meet modern business demands. The Source‑to‑Pay framework provides exactly that foundation. By addressing visibility gaps, eliminating manual processes, strengthening supplier engagement, and integrating data, organisations unlock long‑term efficiency and competitive advantage.

S2P excellence is ultimately about transforming procurement from a cost centre into a strategic enabler—one that delivers measurable outcomes across the entire business.

Tactical Recommendations for Advanced Practitioners

  1. Standardise supplier data models across sourcing, contracting, and invoicing to reduce integration friction and improve analytics quality.
  2. Build a governance framework around spend visibility, ensuring each category owner has clear KPIs and accountability.
  3. Prioritise automation in approval flows to eliminate bottlenecks and increase process compliance.
  4. Integrate supplier performance dashboards into regular business reviews to strengthen collaboration and continuous improvement.
  5. Align procurement analytics with finance and risk functions for enterprise‑level decision‑making.

entry.title: Trigger Campaign Based on Customer Specific Link Clicks in SAP Emarsys
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a client requirement and would like to understand if Emarsys supports the following functionality within its Automation Center:

Requirement:
The client wants to trigger a new email campaign based on a contact’s specific link click in a previously sent email campaign. This logic needs to be incorporated while designing the automation program.

Scenario:

  • A new contact enters Emarsys and is added to a Welcome Journey automation.
  • If the contact clicks on a particular link in  email during this journey, the next step should be to trigger additional email campaigns automatically from Emarsys.
  • Essentially, the customer journey should dynamically continue based on specific link click interactions.

Could you please confirm the following?

  • Is it possible to use contact-specific link clicks as triggers within Emarsys automation flows?
  • If yes, how can this be configured within Automation Center or any other Emarsys module?
  • Are there recommended best practices or examples for implementing such event-driven campaign triggers?
  • In case this is not supported natively, what alternative approaches are suggested?

Looking forward to your guidance on enabling this requirement in Emarsys.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM APIs: What’s new in 2025 ?
entry.content:
In today’s digital landscape, APIs are the silent engines powering nearly every modern application experience. They connect systems, automate processes, and create the flexibility businesses need to innovate quickly. For SAP Cloud ALM, APIs are far more than a technical add‑on — they’re the foundation that allows customers and partners to integrate, extend, and orchestrate their ALM processes with precision. Whether it's syncing project data, triggering automated workflows, or reacting to real‑time events, APIs open the door to a more connected and intelligent ecosystem. In short: without APIs, SAP Cloud ALM wouldn’t be the adaptable, integration‑ready platform that teams rely on today.

 

 

In this blog post, we will share all new and updated APIs of SAP Cloud ALM in 2025.

 

Transactional APIs

Transactional APIs are REST or OData APIs allowing to read/create/update/delete SAP Cloud ALM resources (Task, Feature...).

New APIs

  • Process Hierarchy
  • Features
  • Test Cases 
  • Documents

Updated Apis

  • Tasks:
    • New attribute "owner", "ownerName", "impact", "probability", "mitigationStrategyId", "categoryId" for Task entity.
    • New API to read, assign and delete feature assignment of task
    • New API to read, assign and delete document assignment of task.
    • New sub status "In Planning" for task type "Requirement".
    • New attribute "timeboxId".
    • Support quality gate and checklist items
    • New attributes "responsibleUserType", "answer", "answerOptions" and 5 custom fields.
    • New attribute "tags"
    • New query parameters "tags" to task API
    • New "updateTags" API endpoint for updating tag assignments
    • New attributes "displayId", "responsibleTeamId", "source" and "externalReferences".
  • Projects:
    • New API to fetch, create, and update program
    • New API to assign and unassign program from project
    • New API to fetch, create, and update deployment plan;
    • New API to create, fetch, update and delete system groups;
    • New API to fetch and update project.
    • New property "userEmail" for User entity.
    • New API to update members in a team role.
    • New property "phaseId" for Project entity, indicates current phase of the project.

Support API

Support APIs are REST APIs allowing to interact with the SAP Support Backbone: read/create/update SAP cases.

  • ITSM

Workflow for API

Workflow API is a REST API to trigger and interact with external workflow systems or services.

  • Workflow for Feature

What's next ?

Our goal is to provide APIs covering all SAP Cloud ALM resources. Missing APIs should be released in coming quarters. In case you see a gap, the customer influence portal should be used to describe your needs under the "External API Management" category.

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Welcome to the SAP Community Group – Pune! 🚀
entry.content: Dear All, happy to associate and grow the SAP community in Pune and looking forward to have more and more events in the city.

entry.title: Re: 3451944 - FI PL : KSeF number enablement for MIRO pos...
entry.content:
Hi cdimba,

In my case, I implemented this note and had the problem that the KSEF number disappeared from the field (SAP ECC).

I think it's a problem with the way that SAP calls the dynpros. My solution was to condition the code of the 'PUT_DATA_TO_SCREEN_OBJECT'  method so that it only runs if the dynpro is the new one you created for the note, something like:

if sy-dynnr = '0100'.

me->if_ex_badi_fdcb_subbas04~invfo = im_invfo.

endif.

entry.title: Authorization does not work together with gigya.account in the external.generic step_CDC Dataflow
entry.content:
We asked about a problem with authorisation token inside this URL:

Solved: Authorisation not working in external.generic step... - SAP Community

We managed to get it to work and the external.generic is working BUT without gigya.account connected to it.

If we disconnect gigya.account from external.generic - it's taking bearer token from shared variable but sends nothing.

If we connect gigya.account with external.generic - it's not taking bearer token from shared variable and we are not able to continue.

If we connect gigya.account with external.generic and copy-paste bearer token directly inside authorisation value field - it's working. We can see that external.generic is authorised and is sending data.

 

What can we do to use shared variable and gigya.account at the same time? Is there any other option?

entry.title: No corresponding API was found to publish material standard costs
entry.content: No corresponding API was found to publish material standard costs

entry.title: Re: Help needed for a custom POD plugin

entry.title: Testing Cloud Integration Groovy 4
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I wanted to check with you about an issue I’m not able to overcome. I watched your video on the topic “Future-proof your scripts in SAP Cloud Integration” by @MortenWittrock on DevOctoberFest, interesting one

In the past, I have been using the Exchange object for several things, and I’d like to future-proof those scripts, but I haven’t been able to get it working.

In my tenant, I’m testing this very simple Groovy script:

// This Groovy Flowstep Version 2, running with Groovy 4, Downgrade the script if older behaviour needed.

import com.sap.it.script.v2.api.Message
import com.sap.it.script.v2.api.proxy.Exchange

def Message processData (Message message)
{
Exchange e = message.exchange
message.setProperty("ExchangeName",e.getContext().getName())
message.setProperty("GroovyVersion",GroovySystem.version)
return message
}

This is exactly the same script used in the demo (Min 27 Second 17), but in my tenant, even after upgrading the Groovy version for this script, it’s throwing the following exception (during simulation and also in runtime):

unable to resolve class com.sap.it.script.v2.api.proxy.Exchange
@ line 4, column 1.
import com.sap.it.script.v2.api.proxy.Exchange

Am I missing something? I’m finding it difficult to leverage this Groovy 4 feature, and I couldn’t find much information on the provided classes either.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Mikel

entry.title: Should the standard BAdI be activated as is?
entry.content:
Dear team,

We are proceeding with the standalone retrofit configuration in accordance with the guide below, and in the course of that process, we have reached the step where the BAdI RETRO_STANDALONE needs to be activated.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/a59a7a7e736a4815bd927c2d48a00eec.html
Change Control Management -> Dual Landscape Synchronization (Retrofit)Standalone Retrofit Step 5.
"Implement the Business Add-In CTS_EXPORT_FEEDBACK in accordance with SAP Note 2179147 Information published on SAP site."

SAP Note 2179147 
https://me.sap.com/notes/2179147

According to the guide and the SAP Note, it appears that the classic standard BAdI RETRO_STANDALONE should be activated directly in SE19. Is this correct?
Another team member has suggested that, instead of activating the standard BAdI, we should first copy it under a different name as a custom BAdI and then activate that. As a result, we are unsure whether we should activate

entry.title: How to find the logic where the particular value is changed for dynamic variable in debugger.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I got an issue to find out the logic where the invoice type is getting changed from 12 to 4. Here, we don't know the variable name and watch point cannot be used. So, I have debugged the issue using debug layer and got the logic in custom code where it took bit long time .
But I would like to know the better way to debug the issue. Could you please help me on it.

entry.title: Re: updateOnNull in Learning
entry.content: Hi @rhona Please refer to the KBA 3512178 to see if it helps with the UpdateOnNull configuration.

entry.title: Re: Exchange Rate Conversion in I_EngmntProjFcstCube_2
entry.content:
Hello SVops,

1.CDS View for Currency Conversion: If you are looking for Plan costs and Actual costs in Company code currency , you may refer to the Query C_EngmtProjMargAnlysQuery which has the Cost and Revenue displaying in the Company code currency. If this does not complete your requirement, you may use the cube I_EngmntProjSalesMarginCube by building a custom query on top of this and proceed.

For more information on CDS views for Professional services, please refer this help docu https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/6340bec670a24801a514d691912482bf.html?locale=en-US&version=2508.500 

2. Exchange Rate Type and Timing: 
Plan values: For customer project planned cost calculation, the currency conversion uses the exchange rate based on the1st of fiscal period (of the month of planning). The exchange rate type applied for this calculation is P.

Actual values: This uses the exchange rate type M.

ETC (Estimate to Complete) : This is calculated using the planned cost rate for their calculation which is planned cost amount / planned quantity.

EAC (Estimate at Completion): This is calculated with the formula ETC + Actual cost upto Fiscal period.

All of the above calculation for ETC and EAC is available in this KBA note https://me.sap.com/notes/3617498

Documentation for Exchange rates: Please refer https://me.sap.com/notes/2930207

More documentation on Professional services: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/b102965653691e5ee10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US&version=2508.500 

Hope this helps!

Thanks,

Taarikha C S

entry.title: Re: Disable custom action button when a field value changed in SAP Fiori Elements
entry.content: I have a field in the object page that is coming from the cds view. When in Edit Mode, based from the field value when changed to blank, the custom action button (that is created from BAS using add custom button in Show Page Map; not from CDS annotation) will be disabled and when it has value populated, the custom action button is enabled.

entry.title: Re: How to disable, delete and edit buttons function in attachment list.
entry.content:
Hi there, is there any way to exclude the “New” button and disable only the “Edit” and “Delete” buttons in the attachment list? I've already tried with the method of copying the standard class CL_GOS_ATTACHMENTS into a custom one, with the method of SET_TOOLBAR but it doesn't getting called at all. 

Also, I’d like to ask if there is any way to exclude these changes outside of transaction code MM03. I’ve tried using a check in CHECK_STATUS like:

IF sy-tcode = 'MM03'.
 AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'OBJNAME1' ID 'ID1' FIELD 'FIELD1'.
  IF sy-subrc is NOT INITIAL. 
    on_mode_change( 'D' ).
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

However, with this check, attachment list buttons are available for use. Any thoughts?

entry.title: Filters 'consider intraday reconciliation results' and 'consider end-of-day reconciliation results'
entry.content:
• From the public cloud edition 2602 the filter options 'Consider Intraday Reconciliation Results' and 'Consider End-of-Day Reconciliation Results' are missing in the Cash Flow Analyzer app.
  • How to enable the filter options 'Consider Intraday Reconciliation Results' and 'Consider End-of-Day Reconciliation Results' in the Cash Flow Analyzer app.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Filters 'consider intraday reconciliation results' an...
entry.content:
With the public cloud edition 2602, the filter options 'Consider Intraday Reconciliation Results' and 'Consider End-of-Day Reconciliation Results' require enabling the 'New Reconciliation Model' in the configuration activity 'Define Basic Settings'.

To enable:

  1. Navigate to the configuration activity 'Define Basic Settings' (SSCUI ID 101025).
  2. Select the checkbox 'Enable New Reconciliation Model'.
  3. Save the changes.

entry.title: Re: How to migrate universe on 3rd parties DB to HANA in BI4.2
entry.content: Hello Matthew_Shaw,

entry.title: Re: Understanding Your Partner Support Performance with the help of SAP for Me Reporting
Re: SAP S/4 Hana - Cross Company and Inter-Company Transactions
entry.content: Very nice document.  Thanks Manoj.

entry.title: Re: Perform Maintenance Job without assigned technician,...
entry.content:
Good day @afernandez 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Would it be possible to do time confirmation of an order operation against a Work Center instead of a technician?

Answer: Not as a true work center only confirmation. The public cloud ties the confirmation to a person, in this case a “worker”. The standard “no pre assignment” approach is self assignment from the work center queue, then confirm as the logged in user.

You are getting the error because you are using Quick Confirmation on at least one job that is not assigned to you or not assigned at all. In that case, the app blocks the action, which is exactly the message you see.

Here are a few things the public cloud offers in this case:

  • Time confirmation is always posted by the logged in user that is mapped to a worker or BP. Meaning a worker cannot be the performer of a confirmation by itself. So, “Confirm only at work center level with no technician” is not supported as a pure work center posting. You still need a user worker behind it.
  • Quick Confirmation is restricted when the jobs you select are not assigned appropriately. See Perform Maintenance Jobs for more details.

Here is a quick guide:

In your system, open CBC, then configure your solution. Search for Define Control Parameters to perform Maintenance Jobs. For your Plant and the relevant Type, set the Action to Confirm Jobs Without Assignment, then save and publish if need be.

You need to correct execution in the app; no technician pre assignment. To do this Open Perform Maintenance Jobs, filter to find your jobs, choose Assign to Me and run Quick Confirmation.

Alternatively, you may consider as option B not to use Quick Confirmation. For this, open the Job, then use Confirm from inside the job details. The reason is, Quick Confirmation is the part that is strict about assignment.         

To summarise:

  • Work center only confirmation is not supported
  • Make use of Confirm Jobs Without Assignment to allow the flow but Quick Confirmation still requires proper assignment handling
  • It is advisable to self assign from the team’s queue and confirm time.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Valid to date
entry.content:
Hi @U415486 ,
With reference to the question you asked, please find the answers below with bit of explanation.

Answer 1. BOM Component Valid-To Date Behavior, Blank Valid-To Date, SAP interprets a blank Valid-To date as open-ended validity. This means the component is valid until explicitly changed or deleted.
It is standard behavior when no expiration date is maintained during BOM creation. 31.12.9999 or Custom Dates When a BOM is created or changed with a validity period, SAP populates the Valid-To field with either The System default maximum date (31.12.9999) if no specific end date is given or Custom date if the user or engineering change number specifies an expiration.

Answer 2. Valid-To Date Discrepancy Between CS03 and STPO.
CS03 (Transaction Display) , Always shows 31.12.9999 as the default Valid-To date unless a specific end date is maintained. It calculates validity dynamically based on change numbers and system rules.
SAP Notes (e.g., 2611243, 3058214) confirm that CS03 does not directly read STPO/STKO fields but derives validity at runtime.

STPO (Database Table)
Stores the raw Valid-To field exactly as entered (blank, 31.12.9999, or custom). Blank values here mean “valid indefinitely,” but CS03 will still display 31.12.9999. This explains the discrepancy: CS03 is presentation logic, STPO is storage logic.

Which Source Is Correct?
For reporting and programmatic checks, rely on STPO/STKO tables because they store the actual maintained values. CS03 is useful for user-facing display but not for technical reporting.

Blank Valid-To = valid indefinitely (standard SAP behavior). CS03 shows 31.12.9999 by design; STPO stores the actual field. For reporting, use STPO/STKO with date checks against SY-DATUM to filter active BOM components.

Hope you got better answers to your questions.

entry.title: New SAP for Me Services & Support Overview: A Fresh UX for a Better User Experience
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the successful go-live of the newly redesigned SAP for Me Services & Support dashboard overview tab! This marks a significant milestone in our ongoing journey to listen to your feedback and deliver a more seamless, efficient, and intelligent support.

What’s New? A Modern, User-Centric Dashboard

The new Services & Support dashboard overview tab brings a host of improvements designed with your needs in mind:

  • Prominent, High-Visibility Search: Instantly find the support knowledge you need with a streamlined, central search bar.
  • Holistic, Actionable Cards: Core use cases like managing cases, service requests, and live recommendations are now surfaced in visually distinct cards for quick access.
  • Quick Access Card: The Customer Interaction Center (CIC) is now part of the “Quick Access Card”, putting essential resources at your fingertips.
  • Unified Case Management: You now have a single, more intuitive “Manage Cases” card that puts case management and creation at your fingertips.
  • Simplified Navigation: The dashboard layout is cleaner, with concise actions and a simplified “below the fold” area, making it easier to focus on what matters most. 

Why This Matters: Benefits at a Glance

This is more than just a visual update - it’s a strategic step toward a unified, intelligent support experience:

  • Consistent Digital Experience: Enjoy a harmonized look and feel across SAP for Me boosting confidence.
  • Faster Problem Resolution: Streamlined workflows and improved visibility help you resolve issues more quickly.
  • Improved Customer Satisfaction: A modern, intuitive interface means more successful outcomes.
  • Efficiency Gains: Higher self-service rates and reduced redundancy free up your time for what matters most.

The Road Ahead: The Intelligent Front Door

This launch is just the beginning. Our vision is to make SAP for Me the “intelligent front door” for all your post sales SAP interactions: a unified entry point powered by conversational and agentic AI, to deliver effortless navigation, advanced self-service, personalized support, and fast task execution. Stay tuned for more updates as we continue to innovate and lead in digital customer experience!

We invite you to explore the new dashboard and share your feedback. Together, we’re shaping the future of SAP support – one step at a time.

entry.title: Re: Is ADS stateless?
entry.content:
The call to ADS to return a PDF is a 2 step interaction. First, the ERP system calls the ADS HTTP API, and send the data. The connection is closed.

Then, the ADS systems calls back to the ERP system, do the logon and send back the PDF data. That's why you need to create an user on both sides and configure the connection on the JAVA side to the ERP system.

When you put a WD on front of the ADS, the WD cannot rewrite the headers, the source origin should remain untouched in order to the ADS knows how to do the call back.

entry.title: Re: ABAP OOP
entry.content:
Hello

First, yes, ABAP OOP can be used on real projects, but it always depends on what you want to do.

I would recommend you to start from the following course: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquiring-core-abap-skills

Then, you could go further with ABAP Cloud and RAP topics

Additionally, you will need to focus on clean core principles. Here is a resource: https://www.sap.com/documents/2022/10/52e0cd9b-497e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Best regards

I did not receive the SMS confirmation code, and the system shows the message: “We did not send a code because you are not authorized to request phone calls. Please contact the system administrator.”

Since this is a student account, I do not have a system administrator to contact. Could you please advise how I can resolve this issue or how to get support for phone verification?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 3, 2026 from 09:00 - 14:00 local time in São Paulo, Brazil. The language of the event and the content will be English, with some support in Portuguese.

Event Address: SAP Brasil - Avenida das Nações Unidas - Vila Gertrudes, São Paulo - State of São Paulo, Brazil

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: How to add a custom tab in Service Contract Fiori app...
entry.content:
Hi,

I guess you are referring to Manage Service contract application which is a Fiori Element applicaiton.

As you are trying to extend the app, you first need to check the guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/468a97775123488ab3345a0c48cadd8f/a892eb8ae1fb498a9bc6c5194432e820.html?locale=en-US

 

Here is another guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAPUI5/285985e9cf204697aee91214fc19d95c/61cf0ee828824903907464c80dd0d88c.html?locale=en-US&version=1.84

Then, using VSCode or BAS,  the wizard will assist you in the implementaiton. You may also need to extend the CDS view/Annotation

Best regards

entry.title: Re: TM Scheduling of Sales / Purchasing / Delivery Documents with BPS Integration
entry.content:
@luciano_fialho22

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub (Campinas, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, March 2, 2026 in Campinas, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:00 - 14:00. The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch.

The event will be held in English with some support in Portuguese.

The instructor is the Developer Advocate @KRiedelsheimer, @ajmaradiaga  .

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

Please note that seating is limited - we can only accomodate 30 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Bring your coffee and bring your curiosity - SAP BTP vs SAP ABAP
entry.content: Dear All, On this coffee corner, I would like to hear the opinion of fellow members on your views on best practice to be followed while deciding what to move to SAP BTP and what to keep in on-premise SAP ABAP while running a large SAP Transformation program. Your views please.

entry.title: Re: TM Scheduling of Sales / Purchasing / Delivery Documents with BPS Integration
entry.content:
@sivapuram 

I would guess this is somewhere in ATP. TM schedules only from loading on, ew don't know about picking or material availability. So I would propose to check settings in ATP when they derive a material availability in the past.

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP Build low-code/no-code SAP CodeJam focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation, and how to work with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to send those events to your processes.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, March 4, 2026 from 09:00 - 14:00 local time in São Paulo, Brazil (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English with some support in Portuguese.

Event Address: Exed Consulting (SAP AppHaus) R. James Joule, 65, 5º andar, Cidade Monções, São Paulo - SP, 04576-080

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Exed Consulting – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga .

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

BR,

Mohit.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, March 5, 2026 in Rio de Janeiro from 09:00 to 14:00. The language of the event and the content will be in English with some support in Portuguese.

The event is kindly hosted by INFNET Institute. Thank you!

The instructors are Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga  

Location: INFNET Institute - Rua São José - Centro, Rio de Janeiro - State of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: Email notifications should be sent whenever a backgro...
entry.content:
If you mean to monitor the jobs with status aborted, you may configure the Alert Monitor and Alert Management.

Alert Monitor | SAP Help Portal:

  • Background Processing Monitor | SAP Help Portal
  • Forwarding Alerts to Alert Management (ALM) | SAP Help Portal
  • Defining an Automatic Alert Notification | SAP Help Portal

Alert Management (BC-SRV-GBT-ALM) | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM - no supported use cases available for any service
entry.content:
Hello,

we have our Cloud ALM instance and want to get started with it and monitor services like Work Zone, Build Process Automation, Integration Suite, SAP Analytics Cloud, and more. But somehow, none of the 20 services has any supported use case in the Landscape Management. So we can also not add any endpoint because it will say no supported use case requires an endpoint. Not even Cloud ALM itself.

Did we miss something? We are not sure how we could fix this. It looks like in the screenshot for all services.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Electronic Account Statement
entry.content:
Hi @bahattin_orbis ,

I'm afraid I can't help you completely - however, I'd recommend you to have a look at the following help content if you're using the product SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. You might find the info you're looking for there: 

  • Interpretation Algorithms
  • Overview on All Predelivered Processing Instructions
  • Configuration for Bank Statement Processing

Furthermore, I'd recommend you to start using processing rules to process your bank statements. You can create and define your processing rules in the Manage Processing Rules app and select certain processing instructions. For more information, see the following help content and the related links at the end of each page:

  • Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements
  • How to Create Processing Rules

I hope this helps you furhter. 

Best regards,
Anita

entry.title: Re: 2602 upgrade period details - when is Q to P transpor...
entry.content: Hi Silvia, I had looked through those 2 documents, but neither of them indicate when the transports from Q to P are being blocked. Only when the actual upgrade is happening. But the Q to P transports are already being blocked a few days in advance usually. This was aways mentioned in the slide deck of the what's new webinars. But I cannot find these slide decks anymore.

entry.title: How to configure managed approuter to serve CAP endpoints?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a fullstack CAP cloud application along with a managed approuter. I am having some trouble getting the approuter to correctly point to my cap service (currently without a UI). Currently the approuter correctly points to my ui5 page (capbase) but my cap service (dev-capbase-srv) returns the error message 

Cannot GET / 

However, when I append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employees to my url I get a 401 unauthorized error. But I have tested this via postman by generating a bearer token and can then see the data displayed. But my question is why is my approuter not directing me to this service automatically without having to append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employee endpoint to my url: port4004-workspaces-ws-ak3uq.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/

Further information: I have a fiori list report generated in my app folder for this CAP employee service
my xs-app.json file is as follows:

{
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/v4/catalog/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/v4/catalog/$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "csrfProtection": false,
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ],
  "welcomeFile": "/interfacebase"
}

mta.yaml 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: capbase
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
- name: capbase-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: gen/srv
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    instances: 1
    memory: 256M
  build-parameters:
    builder: npm-ci
- name: capbase
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app/router
  requires:
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: capbase-html5-runtime
  - name: capbase-destination-service
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: false
      name: ui5
      url: https://ui5.sap.com
  provides:
  - name: app-api
    properties:
      app-protocol: ${protocol}
      app-uri: ${default-uri}
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    memory: 256M
- name: capbase-app-deployer
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: gen
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: srv-api
  - name: capbase-html5-repo-host
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: app/
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - interfacebase.zip
      name: interfacebase
      target-path: app/
    - artifacts:
      - employeeui.zip
      name: employeeui
      target-path: app/
- name: interfacebase
  type: html5
  path: app/interfacebase
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
- name: employeeui
  type: html5
  path: app/employeeui
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: capbase-auth
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      oauth2-configuration:
        credential-types:
        - binding-secret
        - x509
        redirect-uris:
        - https://*~{app-api/app-uri}/**
      tenant-mode: dedicated
      xsappname: capbase-${org}-${space}
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-plan: application
  requires:
  - name: app-api
- name: capbase-html5-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-host
- name: capbase-html5-runtime
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-runtime
- name: capbase-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: capbase-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm ci
    - npx cds build --production

entry.title: 2026 Copenhagen Community Event Coordination Discussion
entry.content:
This discussion board is meant to brainstorm, share ideas, requests, for coordinating event in Denmark.

We have received request to include other cities in the group and I believe is a great opportunity to make road trip to other cities in DK.

We have a lot of ambitions for 2026, specially to structure a SAP Insider track event, with presentations, live content and networking opportunities.

I hope we can have the first social event on the other side of the winter holiday, between week 9-11.

Share your thought and ideas 📢

entry.title: WebI - Calculate the sum of daily average values across a specifc date range
entry.content:
Hello WebI Gurus,

I need to report some SAP Data where for a single date several time values / time bookings exist (the first "Stunden" column in the table below). This data should be summed per date.

The second time value column is a join from a worktime per day table -> only one value for each date exists -> same value for every time booking record is joined. (second "Stunden" column in the table below).
This value I need in the report is  average per date.

In my Webi Report I got a summarized table (aggregating the data across all dates) on the left and a table showing the data per date on the right.

Right table is working correctly - strangely projection average seems to be applied although I switched in IDT business layer to from average to none.

Left table displays same data even if date is not in drill down.
When I use =Sum([Soll Stunden])  I get #MULTIVALUE error.
I tried function =Average([Soll Stunden]) In ([Datum])  as variable but this delivers the average across all dates and not the average per day summed up across all dates.

 

Would really appreciate any help in regards how to solve this issue.

Thanks and best regards
Axel

entry.title: Going from gATP to AATP, is there an equivalent for EDQA in BOP?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have made the move going from gATP to AATP. We created the BOP run etc but we are still missing EDQA (event-driven quantity assignment). Currently we run the BOP very frequently to replicate EDQA however, this results in sales documents being locked frequently (for one second). 

Is there any way to replicate EDQA in AATP without having to run BOP every x minutes? I have looked at the roadmap but I haven't seen any mention for this yet.

Thanks a lot already!

entry.title: Re: SAP Security Configuration Dashboard using SAP Analytics Cloud (Template)
entry.content:
Hi Wihem,

I configured everything as described until Page 22.

When creating the Import Job I get the following error message:

I also cannot open the Import Settings of the Import Jobs, do you have an idea where I could have made a mistake in the configuration?

 

Best regards,

Michaela

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Architecture Guide
entry.content:
At the risk of stating the obvious but when it comes to ETL (a moniker not found on this page) SAP offers this

  • SDI (Roadmap only entries of supporting different versions of connector systems like Db2 etc)
  • SLT (Roadmap last entry 2023)
  • Edge Integration Cell (not really a ETL but a process driven local integration runtime of Cloud Integration / Integration Suite - Costing an arm and a leg for mass data replication with 0.5 x messages of regular Cloud Integration)

That's it folks, or did I miss something essential?

entry.title: Re: Production Integration with Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
@Christian_Ries excellent compilation of options for mapping production material requirements.
Thank you very much!

However, I have I very specific question regarding the creation of warehouse orders and warehouse tasks for production material requests.

I have a scenario related to “Extended Production Integration - 6NI” with the following settings:
Single order staging and calculation based on PMRs

If I now create two production orders, e.g., 500000 (PRM = 910000000000) and 500001 (PRM = 910000000001) and then use the “Create Warehouse Tasks - Staging for Production” app to create the warehouse tasks, the system bundles the warehouse tasks for different PMRs into one warehouse order.

However, in my case, this is explicitly not what I want. I would like to receive a single warehouse order per PMR with several warehouse tasks of the same PMR.

I want to know A) why the system bundles several PMRs into one warehouse order in single order staging and B) what I need to set or configure in the system to define the PMR as a split criterion for warehouse order creation?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Willkommen!
entry.content:
Herzlich willkommen an alle Interessierten aus dem Raum Basel (CH), aus der Region Freiburg (DE) und natürlich auch aus dem schönen Elsass!

Wir möchten einen offenen, ungezwungenen SAP-Stammtisch aufbauen und laden Entwickler, Berater und Nutzer ein, sich regelmässig mit uns zu treffen – abwechselnd in Basel und in Freiburg. Im Mittelpunkt steht der lockere Austausch rund um SAP: miteinander ins Gespräch kommen, Fragen stellen, Erfahrungen teilen, voneinander lernen und sich vernetzen.

Ob ihr etwas essen möchtet oder einfach nur auf ein Getränk vorbeikommt, bleibt ganz euch überlassen. Wichtig ist uns eine entspannte Atmosphäre, in der sich jede und jeder wohlfühlt. Kommt vorbei, bringt gerne weitere Interessierte mit und gestaltet diesen SAP-Stammtisch gemeinsam mit uns!

Alle, die die Idee des offenen Austauschs und der Zusammenarbeit teilen, sind eingeladen, sich einzubringen, Themen vorzuschlagen oder zukünftige Events mitzugestalten.

Grüsse Marcel und Bastian

entry.title: Entry opportunities into SAP for experienced leaders from technical backgrounds
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have led teams of up to 100 people in technical and craft-based environments and have strong experience in operations, processes and day-to-day business workflows.

I don’t have SAP hands-on experience yet, but I’m highly motivated to learn and invest the time needed to build solid fundamentals. Are there entry paths or programs at SAP or within the SAP ecosystem for people with this kind of background who want to seriously transition into SAP?

 

Any guidance or experience would be appreciated.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Why Source‑to‑Pay Excellence Defines Modern Procurement Leadership
entry.content: Good one 👍

entry.title: 1. SAP Stammtisch in Basel am 29.01.2026
entry.content:
SAP interessiert dich – beruflich oder privat – und du hast Lust auf Austausch in lockerer Runde?
Dann komm zu unserem neu gegründeten SAP-Stammtisch Basel-Freiburg in Basel vorbei!

Wir freuen uns auf einen spannenden Abend und viele interessante Gespräche!
Marcel und Bastian

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - Management of Classic Stories Before Deprecation
entry.content:
Hi @Galin_Manolov ,

there should be an information attached to the sample package stating that it will no be updated anymore. There will be new Sample Content in SAC including Story, Model and Theme in 2026 QRC2.

Thanks,

Thomas

entry.title: Re: errors coming in the ui5 walkthrough yaml file for th...
entry.content: I created the destination in the BTP but that did not solve the issue. It seems the migration of the project to run in the Fioridev workspace is not necessary; so, I deleted the project from the workspace  and recreated it. It solved the issue. Now, need to do the routing chapters.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Mobile Services - problem with file and ima...
entry.content: In the destination section, the maximum request size (bytes) it lets me set is 1048576. My idea is to be able to upload images and PDFs without having problems with sizes. The limit is 10 MB. It is NOT on the backend side. I think I saw it in the mobile service logs section.

entry.title: Re: Unable to search a document by candidate name after migration from onboarding1.0
entry.content: My company recently completed the migration, and we’ve encountered the same issue. All documents that migrated as version 1.0 are showing the admin user as the owner, and we aren’t able to search those documents by the original candidate's name. I’ve tried searching multiple variations of both the person and user names, but the results come back empty—even though I can see that they have documents listed under version 1.0. Did you end up opening an SAP ticket for this issue? If so, were they able to resolve it for you?

entry.title: Re: Create a custom MDF Object with multiple records
entry.content:
Receiving error as "No permission"

We do not want to use Manage Data for saving data.

once in people profile , data is entered it gives error as "No permission":

All object permission is enabled in role

entry.title: Re: Update to header in Onboarding 2.0
entry.content: Thank you so much for the comment. Do you have any other tricks or tips that would allow us to add a phone number somewhere on that screen that would help our TA team?

entry.title: Re: Is SAP still investing and supporting B1if going forw...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

SAP Business One Integration Framework remains an important component and tool for SAP Business One product and partners & customers. It has several key benefits in integration efforts, such as:

  • Simple to install and runs on SAP HANA and Microsoft SQL Server
  • Can integrate SAP and non-SAP  systems,  web and cloud-based applications, and services
  • Including message and error handling, business process control, tracking, logging, transaction control, security
  • Uses established integration standards such as XML and XSLT

We are further investing into the solution and ensuring the tool's high security and quality.  Please see the slide from official SAP Business One product roadmap related to the planned Integration Framework innovations:

https://www.sap.com/products/erp/business-one/features.html?pdf-asset=484c875d-c37c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa&page=1

Peter DOMINIK

Global Head of SAP Business One Product Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Integration into Joule
entry.content: @AshrafMohammed : Thank you. We are working on a POC to integrate SuccessFactors with SAP Analytic Cloud and further connect SAP Analytic Cloud to Joule. Our SF tenant is a customer instance but BDC is hosted in TDD.  could you please confirm if integration is possible between these two environments.
